notes.text:
An update is available for java-17-openjdk.
This update affects Rocky Linux 8.
A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE list
notes.title: Topic

notes.text:
The java-17-openjdk packages provide the OpenJDK 17 Java Runtime Environment and the OpenJDK 17 Java Software Development Kit.

Security Fix(es):

* JDK: Enhance array handling (CVE-2025-21502)

Bug Fix(es):

* The Rocky Enterprise Software Foundation OpenJDK packages rely on the copy-jdk-configs package to transfer configuration files to a new updated JDK. With this update, the JDK has been updated to use the latest version of copy-jdk-configs, which has been used for some years on later Rocky Linux versions (Rocky Linux-73867, Rocky Linux-73871, Rocky Linux-73872).

For more details about the security issue(s), including the impact, a CVSS score, acknowledgments, and other related information, refer to the CVE page(s) listed in the References section.

This advisory is provided by Ctrl IQ, Inc for Rocky Linux systems. For more information about CIQ security practices, visit https://ciq.com/security.
notes.title: Description

references.summary: Red Hat CSAF document for RHSA-2025:0422

document.title: Moderate: java-17-openjdk security update for Rocky Linux 8.6, 8.8, 8.10, 9.4 and 9.5
